context: Tests whether the agent generates the message_id once outside the retry loop, retries with jittered backoff, never mutates the envelope on retry, and documents the producer/receiver split.

checklist.name: createMessage uses crypto.randomUUID()
checklist.description: createMessage sets message_id to crypto.randomUUID() (or another standard UUID v4 source) — not Math.random, Date.now alone, or a sequential counter

checklist.name: createMessage envelope shape
checklist.description: createMessage returns an object with schema_version, message_id, sent_at, type, and payload

checklist.name: sent_at uses Date.now()
checklist.description: createMessage's sent_at is Date.now() (or equivalent ms timestamp) captured at creation

checklist.name: publishWithRetry passes same envelope each attempt
checklist.description: Inside the retry loop, each pubnub.publish call passes the original envelope object (does not reconstruct or merge); no message_id is regenerated mid-loop

checklist.name: Loop bounded by maxAttempts
checklist.description: Loop runs at most opts.maxAttempts times (default 5)

checklist.name: Jittered backoff between attempts
checklist.description: On failure, code computes a delay as random * min(maxMs, initialMs * multiplier^attempt) and awaits it before the next attempt (full jitter)

checklist.name: No envelope mutation
checklist.description: publishWithRetry does NOT assign to envelope.message_id, envelope.sent_at, or any other envelope field anywhere in its body

checklist.name: Re-throws after final attempt
checklist.description: After the final failed attempt, the function re-throws the last caught error rather than returning silently

checklist.name: publishOnce composes correctly
checklist.description: publishOnce calls createMessage then publishWithRetry with the returned envelope

checklist.name: Comment on once-generated id
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly states message_id is generated once at envelope creation and never inside the retry loop

checklist.name: Comment on receiver dedup
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly states this producer pattern depends on receivers deduping by message_id (receivers are responsible)
